description_long: This mud patch is a roughly square, low-lying area of dark, moist earth with a slightly uneven surface. The texture appears soft and somewhat clumpy, with subtle variations in color indicating areas of wetter and drier mud. Small patches of green vegetation are faintly visible at the edges, suggesting some grass or moss intrusion. The boundaries of the patch are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with no sharp demarcation. There are a few shallow depressions and minor surface undulations, but no deep holes or standing water. The overall appearance is typical of a well-trodden, urban mud patch found in parks, roadside verges, or near sidewalks after rain.
description: A low, irregular patch of dark, moist mud with subtle green edges and a soft, uneven surface. The boundaries blend gradually into the surrounding ground.
description_view_0: From this angle, the mud patch appears mostly flat with some visible green vegetation at the near edge and a slightly mottled surface.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the darker, wetter center of the patch and shows the gradual blending of the mud into the white surrounding area.
description_view_2: Here, the patch looks more uniformly dark, with less visible green and a subtle rise at the far edge.
description_view_3: This angle highlights a few shallow depressions and the irregular, fuzzy boundary of the mud patch, with hints of green at the lower edge.
attribute_location: urban park pathway or roadside verge
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with no sharp or well-defined edges.
